root_text.an6.42:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena icchānaṅgalaṁ nāma kosalānaṁ brāhmaṇagāmo tadavasari.
root_text.an6.42:1.3: Tatra sudaṁ bhagavā icchānaṅgale viharati icchānaṅgalavanasaṇḍe.
root_text.an6.42:1.5: “samaṇo khalu, bho, gotamo sakyaputto sakyakulā pabbajito icchānaṅgalaṁ anuppatto icchānaṅgale viharati icchānaṅgalavanasaṇḍe.
root_text.an6.42:1.6: Taṁ kho pana bhavantaṁ gotamaṁ evaṁ kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggato:
root_text.an6.42:1.7: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno …pe… buddho bhagavā’ti.
root_text.an6.42:1.8: So imaṁ lokaṁ sadevakaṁ …pe… arahataṁ dassanaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.an6.42:1.9: Atha kho icchānaṅgalakā brāhmaṇagahapatikā tassā rattiyā accayena pahūtaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ ādāya yena icchānaṅgalavanasaṇḍo tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bahidvārakoṭṭhake aṭṭhaṁsu uccāsaddā mahāsaddā.
root_text.an6.42:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā nāgito bhagavato upaṭṭhāko hoti.
root_text.an6.42:2.2: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ nāgitaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.an6.42:2.3: “ke pana te, nāgita, uccāsaddā mahāsaddā kevaṭṭā maññe macchavilope”ti?
root_text.an6.42:2.4: “Ete, bhante, icchānaṅgalakā brāhmaṇagahapatikā pahūtaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ ādāya bahidvārakoṭṭhake ṭhitā bhagavantaṁyeva uddissa bhikkhusaṅghañcā”ti.
root_text.an6.42:2.5: “Māhaṁ, nāgita, yasena samāgamaṁ, mā ca mayā yaso.
root_text.an6.42:2.6: Yo kho, nāgita, nayimassa nekkhammasukhassa pavivekasukhassa upasamasukhassa sambodhasukhassa nikāmalābhī assa akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, yassāhaṁ nekkhammasukhassa pavivekasukhassa upasamasukhassa sambodhasukhassa nikāmalābhī akicchalābhī akasiralābhī,
root_text.an6.42:2.7: so taṁ mīḷhasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ lābhasakkārasilokasukhaṁ sādiyeyyā”ti.
root_text.an6.42:3.3: Yena yeneva dāni, bhante, bhagavā gamissati, tanninnāva bhavissanti brāhmaṇagahapatikā negamā ceva jānapadā ca.
root_text.an6.42:3.4: Seyyathāpi, bhante, thullaphusitake deve vassante yathāninnaṁ udakāni pavattanti;
root_text.an6.42:3.5: evamevaṁ kho, bhante, yena yeneva dāni bhagavā gamissati, tanninnāva bhavissanti brāhmaṇagahapatikā negamā ceva jānapadā ca.
root_text.an6.42:4.1: “Māhaṁ, nāgita, yasena samāgamaṁ, mā ca mayā yaso.
root_text.an6.42:4.2: Yo kho, nāgita, nayimassa nekkhammasukhassa pavivekasukhassa upasamasukhassa sambodhasukhassa nikāmalābhī assa akicchalābhī akasiralābhī, yassāhaṁ nekkhammasukhassa pavivekasukhassa upasamasukhassa sambodhasukhassa nikāmalābhī akicchalābhī akasiralābhī,
root_text.an6.42:4.3: so taṁ mīḷhasukhaṁ middhasukhaṁ lābhasakkārasilokasukhaṁ sādiyeyya.
root_text.an6.42:5.1: Idhāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi gāmantavihāriṁ samāhitaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:5.3: ‘idānimaṁ āyasmantaṁ ārāmiko vā upaṭṭhahissati samaṇuddeso vā taṁ tamhā samādhimhā cāvessatī’ti.
root_text.an6.42:5.4: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno na attamano homi gāmantavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:6.1: Idha panāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi āraññikaṁ araññe pacalāyamānaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:6.3: ‘idāni ayamāyasmā imaṁ niddākilamathaṁ paṭivinodetvā araññasaññaṁyeva manasi karissati ekattan’ti.
root_text.an6.42:6.4: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno attamano homi araññavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:7.1: Idha panāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi āraññikaṁ araññe asamāhitaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:7.3: ‘idāni ayamāyasmā asamāhitaṁ vā cittaṁ samādahissati, samāhitaṁ vā cittaṁ anurakkhissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.42:7.4: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno attamano homi araññavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:8.1: Idha panāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi āraññikaṁ araññe samāhitaṁ nisinnaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:8.3: ‘idāni ayamāyasmā avimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ vimocessati, vimuttaṁ vā cittaṁ anurakkhissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.42:8.4: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno attamano homi araññavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:9.1: Idha panāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi gāmantavihāriṁ lābhiṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:9.2: So taṁ lābhasakkārasilokaṁ nikāmayamāno riñcati paṭisallānaṁ riñcati araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni;
root_text.an6.42:9.4: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno na attamano homi gāmantavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:10.1: Idha panāhaṁ, nāgita, bhikkhuṁ passāmi āraññikaṁ lābhiṁ cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
root_text.an6.42:10.2: So taṁ lābhasakkārasilokaṁ paṭipaṇāmetvā na riñcati paṭisallānaṁ na riñcati araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni.
root_text.an6.42:10.3: Tenāhaṁ, nāgita, tassa bhikkhuno attamano homi araññavihārena.
root_text.an6.42:11.1: Yasmāhaṁ, nāgita, samaye addhānamaggappaṭipanno na kañci passāmi purato vā pacchato vā, phāsu me, nāgita, tasmiṁ samaye hoti antamaso uccārapassāvakammāyā”ti.

translation_text.an6.42:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at a village of the Kosalan brahmins named Icchānaṅgala.
translation_text.an6.42:1.3: He stayed in a forest near Icchānaṅgala.
translation_text.an6.42:1.4: The brahmins and householders of Icchānaṅgala heard:
translation_text.an6.42:1.5: “It seems the ascetic Gotama—a Sakyan, gone forth from a Sakyan family—has arrived at Icchānaṅgala. He is staying in a forest near Icchānaṅgala.
translation_text.an6.42:1.7: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an6.42:1.8: He has realized with his own insight this world—with its gods, Māras, and divinities, this population with its ascetics and brahmins, gods and humans—and he makes it known to others. He proclaims a teaching that is good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased; and he explains a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. It’s good to see such perfected ones.”
translation_text.an6.42:1.9: Then, when the night had passed, they took many fresh and cooked foods and went to the forest near Icchānaṅgala, where they stood outside the gates making a colossal racket.
translation_text.an6.42:2.1: Now, at that time Venerable Nāgita was the Buddha’s attendant.
translation_text.an6.42:2.2: Then the Buddha said to Nāgita,
translation_text.an6.42:2.3: “Nāgita, who’s making that colossal racket? You’d think it was fishermen hauling in a catch!”
translation_text.an6.42:2.4: “Sir, it’s these brahmins and householders of Icchānaṅgala. They’ve brought many fresh and cooked foods, and they’re standing outside the gates wanting to offer it specially to the Buddha and the mendicant Saṅgha.”
translation_text.an6.42:2.5: “Nāgita, may I never become famous. May fame not come to me.
translation_text.an6.42:2.6: There are those who can’t get the pleasure of renunciation, the pleasure of seclusion, the pleasure of peace, the pleasure of awakening when they want, without trouble or difficulty like I can.
translation_text.an6.42:2.7: Let them enjoy the filthy, lazy pleasure of possessions, honor, and popularity.”
translation_text.an6.42:3.1: “Sir, may the Blessed One please relent now! May the Holy One relent!
translation_text.an6.42:3.2: Now is the time for the Buddha to relent.
translation_text.an6.42:3.3: Wherever the Buddha now goes, the brahmins and householders, and people of town and country will incline the same way.
translation_text.an6.42:3.4: It’s like when the heavens rain heavily and the water flows downhill.
translation_text.an6.42:3.5: In the same way, wherever the Buddha now goes, the brahmins and householders, and people of town and country will incline the same way.
translation_text.an6.42:3.7: Because of the Buddha’s ethics and wisdom.”
translation_text.an6.42:4.1: “Nāgita, may I never become famous. May fame not come to me.
translation_text.an6.42:4.2: There are those who can’t get the pleasure of renunciation, the pleasure of seclusion, the pleasure of peace, the pleasure of awakening when they want, without trouble or difficulty like I can.
translation_text.an6.42:4.3: Let them enjoy the filthy, lazy pleasure of possessions, honor, and popularity.
translation_text.an6.42:5.1: Take a mendicant living within a village who I see sitting immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an6.42:5.3: ‘Now a monastery worker, a novice, or a fellow practitioner will make this venerable fall from immersion.’
translation_text.an6.42:5.4: So I’m not pleased that that mendicant is living within a village.
translation_text.an6.42:6.1: Take a mendicant in the wilderness who I see sitting nodding in meditation.
translation_text.an6.42:6.3: ‘Now this venerable, having dispelled that sleepiness and weariness, will focus just on the unified perception of wilderness.’
translation_text.an6.42:6.4: So I’m pleased that that mendicant is living in the wilderness.
translation_text.an6.42:7.1: Take a mendicant in the wilderness who I see sitting without being immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an6.42:7.3: ‘Now if this venerable’s mind is not immersed in samādhi they will immerse it, or if it is immersed in samādhi, they will preserve it.’
translation_text.an6.42:7.4: So I’m pleased that that mendicant is living in the wilderness.
translation_text.an6.42:8.1: Take a mendicant in the wilderness who I see sitting immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.an6.42:8.3: ‘Now this venerable will free the unfreed mind or preserve the freed mind.’
translation_text.an6.42:8.4: So I’m pleased that that mendicant is living in the wilderness.
translation_text.an6.42:9.1: Take a mendicant who I see living within a village receiving robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an6.42:9.2: Enjoying possessions, honor, and popularity they neglect retreat, and they neglect remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an6.42:9.3: They come down to villages, towns, and capital cities and make their home there.
translation_text.an6.42:9.4: So I’m not pleased that that mendicant is living within a village.
translation_text.an6.42:10.1: Take a mendicant who I see in the wilderness receiving robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an6.42:10.2: Fending off possessions, honor, and popularity they don’t neglect retreat, and they don’t neglect remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an6.42:10.3: So I’m pleased that that mendicant is living in the wilderness.
translation_text.an6.42:11.1: Nāgita, when I’m walking along a road and I don’t see anyone ahead or behind I feel relaxed, even if I need to urinate or defecate.”

variant_text.an6.42:5.3: ārāmiko vā upaṭṭhahissati samaṇuddeso vā taṁ tamhā → ārāmiko vā samaṇuddeso vā sahadhammiko vā tamhā (si ), ārāmiko vā ghaṭṭessati samaṇuddeso vā so taṁ tamhā (si), ārāmiko vā ghaṭṭessati samaṇuddeso vā taṁ tamhā (si, pts1ed), ārāmiko taṁ kiṁ ghaṭessati samaṇuddeso vā (sya-all) 17A8:825
